name: VirtualList
comment:
Efficiently displays large lists by **rendering only visible items** plus a configurable buffer.

It does so by **dynamically measuring** the height of the displayed elements.

If item heights are **known in advance,** consider [`FabienHenon/elm-infinite-list-view`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/FabienHenon/elm-infinite-list-view/latest/) for better performance.


## Usage


### Model Setup

Include `VirtualList.Model` in your app's **model:**

    import VirtualList

    type alias Model =
        { virtualList : VirtualList.Model

        -- other fields
        }

    defaultModel : Model
    defaultModel =
        { virtualList = VirtualList.init

        -- other fields
        }


### Update Function

Include `VirtualList.Msg` in your app’s **update function:**


    type Msg
        = VirtualListMsg VirtualList.Msg

    -- other messages
    update : Msg -> Model -> ( Model, Cmd Msg )
    update msg model =
        case msg of
            VirtualListMsg virtualListMsg ->
                let
                    ( virtualListModel, virtualListCmd ) =
                        VirtualList.update virtualListMsg model.virtualList
                in
                ( { model | virtualList = virtualListModel }, Cmd.map VirtualListMsg virtualListCmd )

    -- other cases


### Rendering

Render the virtual list in your **view:**

    view : Model -> Html Msg
    view model =
        VirtualList.view (renderRow model) model.virtualList VirtualListMsg

    renderRow : Model -> String -> Html Msg
    renderRow model id =
        div [] [ text id ]


# Model & Initialization

@docs Model, init, initWithConfig, Msg, update


# Rendering

@docs view


# Setting and Updating the Items

@docs setItems, setItemsAndRemeasureAll, setItemsAndRemeasure


# Scrolling

@docs scrollToItem, Alignment

unions.name: Alignment
unions.comment:
Defines an item’s **position in the viewport** when scrolled to.


### Variants:

  - **`Top`:** Aligns the item to the top.
  - **`Center`:** Centers the item.
  - **`Bottom`:** Aligns the item to the bottom.

unions.name: Model
unions.comment:
The `Model` stores the **virtual list’s state.** Include it in your application’s model:

    type alias Model =
        { virtualList : VirtualList.Model

        -- other fields
        }

You **create** one with the [`init`](VirtualList#init) function.

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment:
**Messages** used by the virtual list.

**Add** `VirtualList.Msg` to your app’s `Msg` type and handle it in `update`.


    type Msg
        = VirtualListMsg VirtualList.Msg

    -- other messages

values.name: init
values.comment: Initializes a virtual list model with the **[default configuration](VirtualList.Config#default).**

values.name: initWithConfig
values.comment:
Initializes a virtual list model with a **custom configuration.**

    initWithConfig VirtualList.Config.default

You can [**modify**](VirtualList.Config) the default configuration:

    config = initWithConfig VirtualList.Config.default
        |> VirtualList.Config.setInitialHeight 650
        |> VirtualList.Config.setBuffer 10

    initWithConfig config

values.name: scrollToItem
values.comment:
Scrolls to the **specified item** unless it is already visible.

    let
        ( newVirtualList, virtualListCmd ) =
            VirtualList.scrollToItem model.virtualList "item-42" VirtualList.Center
    in
    ( { model | virtualList = newVirtualList }
    , Cmd.map VirtualListMsg virtualListCmd
    )
values.type: VirtualList.Model -> String.String -> VirtualList.Alignment -> ( VirtualList.Model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd VirtualList.Msg )

values.name: setItems
values.comment:
Updates the **list of displayed items**.

Only **new items** are measured; existing ones retain cached heights.

    VirtualList.setItems model.virtualList ids
values.type: VirtualList.Model -> List.List String.String -> ( VirtualList.Model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd VirtualList.Msg )

values.name: setItemsAndRemeasure
values.type: VirtualList.Model -> { newIds : List.List String.String, idsToRemeasure : List.List String.String } -> ( VirtualList.Model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd VirtualList.Msg )

values.name: setItemsAndRemeasureAll
values.comment:
Like [`setItems`](VirtualList#setItems), but **remeasures** all items.

Use this if item heights have **changed.**

    VirtualList.setItemsAndRemeasureAll model.virtualList newIds
values.type: VirtualList.Model -> List.List String.String -> ( VirtualList.Model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd VirtualList.Msg )

values.name: update
values.comment:
**Updates** the virtual list model based on received messages.

**Integrate** this into your `update` function and map the result back to your app’s `Msg`.


    update : Msg -> Model -> ( Model, Cmd Msg )
    update msg model =
        case msg of
            VirtualListMsg virtualListMsg ->
                let
                    ( virtualListModel, virtualListCmd ) =
                        VirtualList.update virtualListMsg model.virtualList
                in
                ( { model | virtualList = virtualListModel }, Cmd.map VirtualListMsg virtualListCmd )

    -- other cases

values.name: view
values.comment:
**Renders** the virtual list.

You **provide** it with

  - a **function** that renders an item given its ID,
  - the virtual list **`Model`,** and
  - the virtual list **message** type on your side.

```
view : Model -> Html Msg
view model =
    VirtualList.view (renderRow model) model.virtualList VirtualListMsg

renderRow : Model -> String -> Html Msg
renderRow model id =
    div [] [ text id ]
```

`renderRow` is executed **lazily.**
values.type: (String.String -> Html.Html msg) -> VirtualList.Model -> (VirtualList.Msg -> msg) -> Html.Html msg

name: VirtualList.Config
comment:
**Configure a virtual list** with settings for size estimation, buffering, and visibility during measurement.

@docs Config, default


# Setters

You can **combine** the following setters with the pipe operator:

    VirtualList.Config.default
        |> VirtualList.Config.setInitialHeight 650
        |> VirtualList.Config.setBuffer 10

@docs setListId, setInitialHeight, setDefaultItemHeight, setShowListDuringMeasurement, setBuffer, setDynamicBuffer


# Getters

You will probably have **no need** to access these values directly.

@docs getListId, getInitialHeight, getDefaultItemHeight, getShowListDuringMeasurement, getBuffer, getDynamicBuffer

unions.name: Config
unions.comment:
Defines the **configuration** for a virtual list, including estimated item heights, buffer size, and visibility settings during measurement.

Start with [default](VirtualList.Config#default) and customize it using setter **functions:**

    VirtualList.Config.default
        |> VirtualList.Config.setInitialHeight 650
        |> VirtualList.Config.setBuffer 10

values.name: default
values.comment: Returns a **default configuration** with reasonable initial values.

values.name: getBuffer
values.comment: Returns the number of **extra items** rendered outside the visible area.

values.name: getDefaultItemHeight
values.comment: Returns the **estimated item height** before measurement.

values.name: getDynamicBuffer
values.comment: Returns whether buffer sizing is dynamic based on **scroll speed.**

values.name: getInitialHeight
values.comment: Returns the list’s **estimated height** before measurement.

values.name: getListId
values.comment: Returns the **list container’s ID.**

values.name: getShowListDuringMeasurement
values.comment: Returns whether the list remains **visible during measurement,** which may affect layout.

values.name: setBuffer
values.comment:
Defines how many **extra items** are rendered outside the visible range for smoother scrolling.

**Default:** `5`

values.name: setDefaultItemHeight
values.comment:
Sets the **estimated item height** before measurement.

Uses the **default** if negative: **Default:** `26`

values.name: setDynamicBuffer
values.comment:
Enables dynamic buffer sizing based on **scroll speed.**

**Default:** `True`

values.name: setInitialHeight
values.comment:
Defines the **estimated list height** before measurement.

Uses the **default** if negative: `500`

values.name: setListId
values.comment:
Sets the ID of the **list container** in the DOM.

Uses the **default ID** if an empty string is provided: `"virtual-list"`

values.name: setShowListDuringMeasurement
values.comment:
Controls whether the list remains **visible during measurement.**

If `True`, it may cause **spacing issues.**

**Default:** `False`
